creator: olivierd@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Chris Rees from comment #3)
> Hm, does this actually work with our bz though?  I have the update ready to
> commit, but have no luck connecting.

Yes, it works, but by defaut bugz (executable), tries to connect to Gentoo's Bugzilla, perhaps, we need to patch bugz/argparsers.py file (search gentoo.org), or provide bugzrc (there's bugzrc.example file), with right "base" URL.

There's also man page, which is not installed.

Here example session (by default encoding is UTF-8):

bugz -b https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/xmlrpc.cgi -u olivierd -p MyP4sswd \
--encoding iso-8859-15 login
 * Using https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/xmlrpc.cgi 
 * Logging in

bugz -b https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/xmlrpc.cgi get 190795
 * Using https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/xmlrpc.cgi 
 * Getting bug 190795 ..
Title       : [PATCH] devel/pybugz: update to 0.10.1

[Attachment] [143531] [Update devel/pybugz to 0.10.1.]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Created attachment 143531
Update devel/pybugz to 0.10.1.

* Update to 0.10.1
* Change master site to GitHub.

[Comment #1] beastie@tardisi.com : 20140608T23:40:50
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Darn, you beat me getting this in....though trying to figure out how to use bugz
to submit kind of delayed things.

The biggest delay was figuring out that I need to specify a status, to get
search to find anything.  Now to figure out what statuses I want to include when
I don't want 'ALL'.

Also its not exactly easy...pretty much all the status codes don't match the
site's documentation.  Or that 'ports' is now 'Ports Tree', and that component
is 'Individual Port(s)'....having dug further in what what the other stuff is.

Guess, I'll just stick with send-pr and/or web interface.

[Comment #2] olivierd@FreeBSD.org : 20140611T17:38:17
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Re-assign to crees@, sorry Chris.

[Comment #3] crees@FreeBSD.org : 20140611T18:45:21
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Hm, does this actually work with our bz though?  I have the update ready to
commit, but have no luck connecting.

bugz -b https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/xmlrpc.cgi logout
 * Using https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/xmlrpc.cgi 
 * logging out
